mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which directly suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.0 percent for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint expansion in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising industrial production signals resilient manufacturing capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, supporting corporate margins and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with higher aggregate household income and sustained consumer spending, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary policy responses, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for federal funds rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing quarterly GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates an economic contraction phase, reducing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of declining GDP and rising unemployment fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In early expansion phases, industrial production above 105.0 signals accelerating manufacturing output that precedes positive earnings revisions, driving positive short-term returns for broad equity indices.
failure_condition: Industrial production above 105.0 fails to generate positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances during early expansion phases, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant after 2020.
